title: potentilla glandulosa f. neomexicana
intro: Potentilla glandulosa f. neomexicana, also known as the New Mexico cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous flowering plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The medicinal properties of Potentilla glandulosa f. neomexicana include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to support kidney function, reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Potentilla glandulosa f. neomexicana is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be found in some traditional medicine systems, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
